data.title: BIG DISAPPOINTMENT
data.text: I am so disappointed with this product.  I ordered them in May to have ready for a late June beach trip.  I purchased one as a gift for a friend joining us, and one for ourselves.  I have a set of the 1st generation and they have been awesome on our camping trips.  When I saw that I could use these with the 1st generation it was a no brainer, having extra battery packs available to rotate while recharging.  Prior to our trip I made sure all battery packs were charged.  When we got out to the beach, they did not work!  The water did not flow.  I charged the battery packs, again.  When I hooked it up to the pump, nothing.  I used my 1st generation battery pack with the new pump and it worked beautifully.  So it was not the pump, it's the battery packs.  Unfortunately I did not try them upon receiving them and I have missed the deadline to return the product.  It is disappointing that they do not work.  Big suggestion, as soon as you receive them, charge them and test drive them.  If they don't work, return them immediately.  Or just purchase a different brand.  I am disappointed since you could switch out the older units with the new ones.  But now I am stuck with non working battery packs.  I can't really give it a star.  The pump works, but what good is that if the battery packs do not work.

Just search it up on Amazon and its a black portable sprayer that shows you how much battery is left for about 39$ and rounding up to 41 using prime with the tax. This sprayer is 47$ with tax. data.title: Good Concept-Poor Execution
data.text: The idea is great here, a portable shower/sprayer that is lightweight and runs off rechargeable batteries.  What doesn't work is the angle that the motor has to sit in a water bucket.  If it's not kept upright, the flow of water stops.  Since it's bottom heavy, it'll fall down sideways.  If all the air bubbles are not out, the pump doesn't work.  The hose is too short.  It's real finicky and not worth the effort and fuss.

data.title: Only shower nozzle works now
data.text: Used the dishwashing nozzle twice and not again - stopped working.  Contacted seller to see if I could buy a replacement nozzle but they refused.  Now can only use the shower nozzle, which has no on/off option.  Wastes water that way.

data.title: Bottomline: the battery requires too much charge babysitting to be useful.
data.text: So the actual pump and system works great when it is working. The problem is with the battery.  They do not maintain a charge in storage. This is in the instruction manual.  This means you must put it out and charge them every 2 months, and not doing that can damage the batteries. If you only use it a few times a year camping or as a backup for power outages you must pull it out and charge it regularly. A better solution would be to use better batteries or give us an option to use over-the-counter batteries.For me, that meant  I was dying my hair and when I went to wash out the dye the on-demand water heater broke so I pulled out this camp shower.I heated water and got a bucket and tried to use it -it failed me right when I needed it. The batteries needed to be charged because it had been in storage for over 6 months without use. Also one battery never charged at all.Bottomline: the battery requires too much charge babysitting.

data.title: Batteries don't seem to last
data.text: I purchased this about 3 months ago, and one of the batteries has already quit working. Each of the batteries has been charged only twice. Unsure how long the other battery will last.

data.title: Low Flow, Batteries Weak
data.text: When at top charge, the flow was a little more than a dribble. The batteries do not hold a charge for long. After a few uses, batteries will not charge at all. I do not recommend.

data.title: Not dependable
data.text: Batteries do not hold charge very long and take a long time to charge.

data.title: Pump keeps stopping and no response with help from seller or amazon
data.text: Pump keeps stopping and will not pump water. BOTH batteries are fully charged. No assistance from seller or amazon. Returning.

data.title: Stopped working after 1 use
data.text: We used it on a camping trip in 2022. The trip was 3 days. We put it away for the season and this year, 2023, it will not charge. Very disappointing.

data.title: Stopped working after 2 months
data.text: I loved this product when it worked. Two months later and only 4 uses the unit has stopped working. Batteries are lighting up but the unit just clicks and dose nothing. Very devastating.

data.title: Mine didn't work
data.text: Disappointed that mine didn't work straight out of the box. I bought it for my upcoming camping trip and unfortunately the one month return period ran out by the time I tried it.

data.title: Batteries batteries die so fast
data.text: Batteries my die super fast but it does have good pressure if there was extra batteries 4 them it would be that bad.

data.title: Poor Durability
data.text: We bought this to shower down the dogs several times a week after each visit to the local beach. It replaced the short-lived RinseKit portable shower system, and it has been a big improvement with superior water pressure and spray duration. Unfortunately, the parts are of poor quality. One of the sprayers failed at 6 months, and one of the batteries stopped working (5.5mm connector failed) at 8 months. It's priced $14 less now, but I would have been willing to pay $50 additional for better durability and a longer product life. This is yet another disposable product that will add to our landfill after less than a year's use. It will now be replaced by a real electric pump powered via a car cigarette charger.

data.title: Dura poco la batería
data.text: Dura poco la batería.

data.title: Disappointed
data.text: I’ve used one of these before which was another brand. Set up & use was the the same, however, the button is awkward and at times hard to push. Also, you receive 2 batteries. First one broke after only a couple uses. Can’t charge, etc. complete loss.

data.title: Worked great twice
data.text: I used it on 2 separate occasions and it worked great those times, then I rinsed, drained and stored it for about 5 months and then was going to use it again but it no longer worked.  It turned on but nothing came out

data.title: It stopped working.
data.text: I bought this product for camping and used it to occasionally wash my car outside and water my plants in the summer.  It wasn't always reliable.  It stopped working after approximately one and a half short seasons.  I loved it when it was working and it was too much money to spend on something that didn't last long enough.  I'm going to try a Nemo Helio and I'm hopeful for a better quality product that lasts several years.  I would appreciate a amazon refund

data.title: Just got around to trying it out but disappointed
data.text: Full disclosure I bought this product March 30th 2021. I did not get around to using it or trying it out or anything until today because I don't have any vacation time to be able to go camping so I was waiting until this next summer to go on my trip and I decided to check out the shower while I had a few minutes since I'm on Christmas holiday or whatever you call it the six inches of snow outside I plugged it in and let it charge. I attached it to the pump put it in a large jar of water the pump not the battery....it ran for literally 3 minutes then died....Not so impressed... if i had been out on my adventures I would have been stinky from lack of shower....

data.title: Pump quit working after a month
data.text: I used it for about a month and then the pump would no longer work.

data.title: Proprietary charging cable - steer clear of this garbage.
data.text: Has a proprietary charging cable rendering the entire unit worthless if you lose it. Just use the accepted Industry standard instead of this garbage that doesn't work.  Do not buy this

data.title: Manual spray isn't the greatest.
data.text: Both batteries burned out after 1 day of use, would not charge. Still waiting , 3 weeks now, for Hailey, at support to ship new ones. Sent pix and everything.Sorry, would not recommend or self promote this set.

data.title: Get What You Pay For
data.text: The first one the pump died after one test. Just a 10 second test. Second one the shower head leaks at the connectors. Let's see what it's like after one use.

data.title: I want to write Kedsum directly to if they can help.
data.text: It seems to work 50% of the time.Frustrating

data.title: Sometimes pump takes a timeout in the middle of a warm shower.
data.text: We went on a three-week van trip and were very excited about this shower kit. When it squirted water, everything was great but even with a full battery and a pot full of water, once in a while nothing came out for while, leaving you standing there cold, full of soap and wondering what to do. It eventually worked on & off again but we haven't used it again. I'm not sure if we received a glitchy unit or if these pumps take time out on all units?

data.title: Battery Broke.
data.text: I've had it for about a month and so far it's ok, except one of the batteries won't charge. It seems to be pretty common. I will contact them and update later.Our shower at home is broken, so this was my solution until it can be fixed.  The water pressure is ok. Its not that strong, but it gets the job done. I have thick hair past my shoulders so it takes a minute to soak and rinse my hair.I would say I get 4 or 5 showers out of one charge, so about an hours worth. Unfortunately one of my batteries isn't working anymore.Overall though, I'm satisfied with the performance of the shower, just not the battery breaking and I had never even charged it yet.  That's pretty bad.

data.title: Stopped working after 3 uses. Wish I could return.
data.text: I bought this to give my large rescue dog a bath outside since the bathtub became an impossible feat. The first 3 times I used it, it solved most of our issues with the dreaded bath time. However despite me storing the batteries fully charged they both stopped working after only 3 uses. Now I know why the return window of time is so brief. Such a shame because it was quite handy.

data.title: Batteries died after one use
data.text: I love this product and it worked great the first time, but now neither of the batteries will charge making the shower useless. Super disappointing considering this is my only method of showering because I live off grid. I’ve looked everywhere trying to message the seller but I can’t find where to. It seems like everyone else had a great experience with customer service so I hope they reach out to me, I’d be happy to provide an order number.

data.title: Not what I thought on power.
data.text: For the money it's not that great. Does what it says but cheapest shower head spray is not as good as should be.

data.title: It’s good but the batteries are really bad
data.text: After one month of use it is really good but my batteries won’t charge anymore the suction power it’s quite good.

data.title: very dissapointing
data.text: It worked fine the first two times we used it.  Then it would only come on sometimes, or you'd have to push the on/off button several times to get it to work.  After about 20 uses, the cheap plastic handle on the nozzle came off and then it completely stopped working.  A total waste of money.  The only reason I gave it two stars is because it had good water pressure when it did work.

data.title: If batteries worked longer it would be a great product
data.text: Out of the box this is a simple and easy to use portable camp shower. The blue light indicator on the battery is a helpful feature to show the battery is running low. Like others have mentioned the major issue is with the rechargeable batteries. Brand new the product only arrived with one chargeable battery- the other one arrived dead and never held a charge. I returned those batteries and the next set only worked for two 10 minute sessions. Now, neither battery holds a charge. I let the batteries fully "die" before charging and only left the batteries plugged into the wall charger for 5-7 hours each time so I do not believe this is a user-charging issue. Would not recommend due to current battery issues.

data.title: It's a leaker
data.text: It leaks at the hose connections. It took over 30 minutes to obtain pressure enough to have a stream of water coming from the showerhead.

data.title: Poor battery quality
data.text: Batterys not charging fully or holding the charge well (supposed to blink 4 times for full charge,  charged for many hours, never achieved that. Since use, charge has not gone higher than 2 blinks)

data.title: batteru wount charge

data.title: Check batteries before using

data.title: Poor battery life

data.title: Can’t really rate this product

data.title: Worked great at first, but didn't last
data.text: Bought this at the first of July to use for doing the dishes while camping. Tested it when it arrived and (after putting the O ring in correctly) it worked incredibly well. Love the kitchen sprayer attachment. Bought a large water container that the pump fit down in, placed batteries, sprayers, and pump all in its own bag and stored it all in the camper for our next trip.Unfortunately when we finally got to go camping at the first of September, the unit failed to work at all. I assumed the batteries had lost their charge, but after charging again until the light turned blue, the unit still would not turn on after clicking the button. Neither battery made a difference. After so many positive reviews, I assume this was just a dud but I don't want to risk another $50 to find out.

data.title: revised review
data.text: The pump stopped working after about a dozen uses so have to change the review rating.  It did well when it worked but there is no explanation why it would just stop.

data.title: Worked for the first 2 days and then it developed a mind of it's own.
data.text: Headline says it all

data.title: Batteries dont hold a charge
data.text: Really liked it at first but within 3 charges of the batteries -they no longer hold a charge. Even the second battery Ive barely used! If they do, its on long enough to fill a water bottle before it dies again. I can take it straight from the charger- use it once to rinse a few dishes and thats it! When it worked, it worked well and was excited to use it camping but now Ill have to find something else.

data.title: Hose comes off shower head  you lose water
data.text: The shower head is nice to save water, however the hose slides on and the pressure of the water blows it off and water pours out.  There is no way to attach a hose clamp because of the connection.  This is not worth the money, the battery lasts for 3 showers but runs out of water that pours out because it blows off.  Need to revamp the design.

data.title: Pump is underpowered - make sure you can hang your bucket/water supply above the shower head
data.text: Basically the pump does not have enough power to place it in a bucket on the ground and hold the shower head or the alternate sprayer above you. Or even a foot or two above the top of your bucket. Where I could hang the bucket up and use the shower head at the same or lower level, it gave a passable amount of water - but barely any better than the non-motorized models offered on Amazon for less. Would suggest considering one of those instead.

data.title: Just Don’t!
data.text: Good pressure, but the batteries are junk.  Both died within two months.  Never had this problem with the older models.  Save your money until the battery problem gets sorted out.

data.title: Lightweight and compact solution, but I don't think its reliable enough to use on a regular basis.
data.text: Very lightweight and compact design. It also comes with a trigger nozzle which is helpful to turn on and off the water flow. Flow rate is good, not super powerful, but enough to rinse off a lather.  However, the pump stopped working after the 3rd time I used this product.  The other problem with the design is the fact that you have to keep the battery from getting wet.

data.title: Ok

data.title: Camp shower

data.title: About what you expect.

If attached to water line, power button hangs down towards bucket.Did come with two batteries however. They last about 45 minutes each on a full charge and will pump the water with no problem. Take about an hour to recharge each.Good flow, until your kidlet drops it several times.Could use a better filter on the pump though. Picks up debris that might blow into bucket.Update...... Save your money. This lasted about 3 months then the batteries completely failed. It comes with two and both died. Will not take a charge at all.

data.title: Perfect Product; Absolute Horrible Shipping
data.text: Over the past 8 years I have used numerous portable bidets/showers and this brand/product has been the best so far in terms of durability and quality. I own two bidet showers at home, which my family uses daily and they both arr 3 years old and are still working great.But recently I ordered a 3rd new one, and it never arrived even after a month. It was marked for 2 day delivery! After 4 delays over 4 weeks, and 3 customer service contacts, I finally decided to give up and go for a different vendor/product! Absolutely horrible shipping experience, lately and its unfortunate they are going to lose a customer, that otherwise would have been lifelong.

data.title: Make sure u have a big pot/container

data.title: Doesn’t fit into 7 gal aqua-tainer cube.

data.title: Works great
data.text: Product is good. Works great. But it was missing the white sprayer. Also if you plan on using a water jug it’s too big. I had to buy a bucket. So keep that in mind.

data.title: Cool product but is finicky and hard to get working
data.text: Once you can get it working, this product can be highly useful. More often than not when I start it, the water won’t circulate and I’ve had to try swapping batteries (fully charged) to see if this helps. It doesn’t. After about three minutes of holding the sprayer over my head with the trigger pulled in, usually I can get a stream of water started. It’s just a huge pain in the ass to not have something that you can turn on and use without it being finicky and difficult every time.

data.title: Mixed bag
data.text: This is okay but fussing with the rechargeable batteries is a pain and I found the water pressure was relatively light. Moreover, battery life was poor. Though many would dismiss country of origin criticism and it was not my primary concern, origin of manufacture is Peoples' Republic of China if that matters to you. All of that said, if I had opportunity to purchase again, in candor but with respect to manufacturer and seller, I would use a cup dipped in a bucket to toss water at myself to acheive better results.

data.title: Great pressure . Battery stopped working after 4 uses
data.text: Loved it !! Worked amazing and the water pressure was great. After 4 uses though one of the batteries stopped working . I tried contacting seller to replace that battery pack and never got a response .

data.title: Does it’s job
data.text: Works good.

data.title: Work great if the water source is close & once you get it to turn on
data.text: I used the shower to wash dishes while camping. Did great with hot water but it always took a few tries to get the pump to turn on. Once on I had to adjust the location of the water source to work.

data.title: Easy to use BUT!!!
data.text: Easy to use but batteries didn't last as long as advertised. The spray heads both worked very well and had reasonable volume. I used this to bathe my dog and it worked as I anticipated but again the batteries ran out before I could complete the wash and a second rinse with conditioner.

data.title: Works great but battery life is not great
data.text: The products works great . Just needs to improve its battery life. Make sure you don't let the water goes low otherwise air would get sucked by the motor . My only complaint is its battery. It could be improved. This product could be devolved and make way more sales .

data.title: Batteries are a problem
data.text: The shower mechanism itself is relatively easy to use and the suction power is just ok. Great for short showers or washing dishes. The battery life is somewhat short so it's good that they supply 2 batteries. The real problem is replacement batteries are hard to find or non-existent. One of the 2 supplied batteries one died completely, and there is only one replacement (not from this vendor) that exists. However, supply has been non-existent so I'm now just down to one battery. No help from the vendor either

data.title: Great Shower for Off-grid
data.text: Purchased this is Jan, it worked great!  We had a shower, with running water in the exact temperature we wanted. We live off grid without running water so this was a great addition for our camp that we live in year round. In July one of the batteries stopped working. The on off button wouldn’t work anymore. Wouldn’t charge. We used the other one until the same thing happened. It seems like something different could perhaps make this shower greater. The on off button could be different. And separate from the battery maybe. I kept the battery dry, even though it had to be close to the shower and running water, but it seemed like normal wear and tear from usage is what caused it to stop working. I see that there is a warranty on the battery but I can’t find the place to replace it! I’d love to order a new one, and I still have the old one except my bf took it apart to make another shower from his boat pump and a boat battery. It works ok but the water pressure is not like this shower. I loved that I could switch shower head to the sprayer and the pump fit right into our seven gallon water holder. This shower is great and we used it everyday and I’m not happy with the replacement we got.

data.title: Returned it
data.text: Te previous version is better. This one had problems. Didn't pump the water, jus6made the noise and the switch is not practical.  Better the other design wit the switch on the unit

data.title: Battery packs are not waterproof. Bad design.
data.text: Literally 2 days after the return window  closed, 1 battery pack is failing, because of poor design.The cable is shorter than the hose, and there's no way 2 hang it anywhere while using it...you are supposed to clip the battery to the hose...where the water is flowing out as you use the sprayer.The water drips down the hose and right to the pack.  I tried hanging it above the hose, but it shortens the reach and just falls down, because there's no hanger on  the battery.Also, it rains where I'm camping...in a rainforest.  Everything is damp.  It's strange to design an electrical device that uses rainwater...and not make it waterproof.Updated 3 stars. It's still going.  Having the 2nd battery helps.  I haven't used it 4 shower yet, just dishes, etc. I don't think it will be able 2 stay dry if used as a shower 2 wash hair, etc. But 4 dishes, it's ok

data.title: Bathing horses....meh
data.text: Bought this to be able to bathe my horse with warm water. Hose definitely needs to be longer. Puts out a lot of water but not much pressure. Ended up using 30 gallons of water. Used a water heater like you use to make hot water for tea, put that in a bucket and mix with hose water to make the water warm. Was a bit tedious but it didn't do too bad of a job. I'll continue using. Wish I could use regular hose attachment but they designed this so you have to use the ones that come with it.

data.title: Battery life
data.text: The battery does not hold charge after using them twice

data.title: Hit or miss
data.text: Update: batteries stopped charging within 30 days but seller sent replacements1 battery dies quickly the other lasts a little longer. The orange shower head can only be used pointing down. The white nozzle is pretty powerful

data.title: Stopped working...
data.text: I really liked the product, used it once almost everyday for 3 to 5 minutes and then put it away until the next day. Worked great for about 2 months. Now it's deader than a hammer...

data.title: Great product horrible phase 2 batteries
data.text: I like the product but the new design for the battery is trash. You charge it. You drive to your camp site that evening the battery is dead without use.

data.title: Water Hose won't stay on pump
data.text: I have  trouble keeping the hose attached to the pump. As soon as I turned it on, the hose would come off the pump squirting water every where. It needs a clamp or something..

data.title: Overall good, but dispenser end leaks like crazy
data.text: Battery and pump are great with good suction.  When I connected the dispenser end, it leaked like crazy which meant I had to constantly turn it on and off.  Consider buying a replacement adaptor to ensure it seals properly.

data.title: Good and bad
data.text: It's really convenient to show when car camping. However, the shower head fall off from the tube after a few uses. I have to cut the tube shorter to have a new tip to hook on the shower head.

data.title: Tubing kinks
data.text: Love the product, the tubing kinks easy and it will not come out.

data.title: I had higher hopes
data.text: I actually rate this a 2.5 as I had higher hopes for this product. I'm currently staying somewhere off-grid that only runs super hot water and so I thought this could be a good solution for me for taking showers without scalding myself. I will say this, it is pretty easy to use and intuitive. Keep in mind that you should make sure you have enough water in your bucket/water holder before you begin your shower. I wish this had an on/off on the actual shower head or water pump as it's awkward the way it is now. The suction cup and shower head holder are really frustrating to use and seems an afterthought. Overall the plastic used in this product is like what you'd find for a child's toy and there are some design misses that make using it a bit frustration. Too bad, this could have been a great solution.  I do think it works well as a "trial run" for those considering how to shower while camping--it made me think I probably want to get one of these portable butane powered showers that run $300-400 when I have the money as that's really what I need and this little guy doesn't cut it for me. best of luck!

data.title: Not very powerful,but it works
data.text: I liked the price and for what it is (Made in China) seems to work for a campsite quickie shower... it is good.

data.title: Worked ok until the hose loosen and attachment cracked.
data.text: Decent pressure and worked well unless you need to move the hose then the nozzle pulls out easily least you with a open hose.  Then after several use the plastic cracked where you screw in the nozzle.  Barely a month.  Tried to look for a replacement attachment but can’t find the part.

data.title: Eh
data.text: I've tried 2 of these. When they actually work you get a great flow but 1 ouuta 3 times it takes fiddling to get it going. For over $40 I have a higher expectation

data.title: Not worth the price
data.text: So it does sort of work. Only one battery actually works. The other battery will pump the water out ona full charge but it takes a while to get it going.

data.title: Great shower, horrible batteries
data.text: We loved this portable shower! It was perfect for what we needed at the time. We took it on a camping trip and it was fantastic to shower and do dishes, and having two batteries worked well. Unfortunately, after that one trip, the batteries started to die very quickly after charging and eventually wouldn't hold any charge. We charged them as directed on the instructions and both of them are inoperable now. I loved this product, but how can we fix the batteries? Is there a guarantee or something? If not, I don't know that I could recommend this product for long-term (more than a month or two) use. The batteries simply won't last long.

data.title: Have a problem in one battery pack
data.text: I charged both battery packs and have a problem with one of them. For some reason it will not stay on. I rechecked the cord connection and I hold the on button several seconds after it starts running but it will not continue to operate when I remove my finger.The battery works well.How can I get some advice or a replacement?

data.title: Works great, when it works.
data.text: Have used this for showering for the past 1.5 years. It works great, most of the time. After about 2 months the cord that attaches to the battery pack stopped working unless it’s bent over 180 degrees. Must be some kind of short in the wire. I haven’t been able to find anything that mentions a warranty. Would love to give this 5 stars. It really works well when it works but when it doesn’t, it’s a cold reminder that I should have bought a different unit. If there was product support I’d love to know about it.

data.title: Non working battery
data.text: The kids shower broke so until we fix it we got this camping shower....After about a few uses one of the batteries stopped working. Kind of upsetting.

data.title: It's okay. Not worth the price.
data.text: We bought this one specifically because it was a battery operated pump system, not a valve and gravity system. We were going to be camping in the desert and couldn't depend on there being trees to hang one of those other shower bags from, so I bought this. I tested it at home, the batteries took a long time to charge but they did charge, but the pump is pretty low power. And because of how it's designed it won't pick up the last inch or so of water, which obviously is a problem. Also, there's a warning saying you can't submerge the batteries, but if you're showering in an enclosed space I'm not sure how you'd keep them dry unless you put a ziplock bag around them. I will say the pressure is okay, but not great. Overall it's a pretty poorly designed product and most definitely not worth $50. Returned it.

data.title: Over priced for what you get
data.text: First off let me say I bought this during hot water line repair at home.Was able to shower with it so it should function for a camping shower. Also used it to wash dogs during repairs at the house. This was actually more usefull than the shower. Biggest issue with shower is the head provided does not have a valve to turn it off, however if it did the pump will cavitate and not resume flow.  So you have to turn it off and on during shower process.Not a good value for the price paid but it is functional.

data.title: 1 battery won't charge
data.text: I'm not sure if the pump is quite strong enough. I know that one of the batteries won't charge.

data.title: It’s ok
data.text: Came with 2 rechargeable batteries and one is them will not work or charge. Came with 2 different shower heads the traditional one is low pressure but sucks up water like crazy, the sprayer saves on water cuz u can drop the water and it has better pressure, not enough pressure to wash a car or atv like it states. Maybe good for plants or Camping dishes or light rinses.

data.title: It worked
data.text: This worked as expected. I'm using it to wash dishes the only thing is when you turn on the pump it does start to leak water out of the nozzle from the pressure. For use and pressure it worked good, and the batteries lasted through 10 gallons of water before having to charge. It took about 2 hours to charge off the generator power.

data.title: cheap plastic but works
data.text: It works okay with enough water pressure. But the battery needs to be recharged often and it takes a long time to recharge them. The plastic housing is cheap, I suspect these will not last long. It meets my need to the most part. Looking at the design of this product, I think I should be able to put together a similar shower by assembling a water pump, and a battery pack.

data.title: works great but
data.text: So I do like this shower/sprayer. It works great with my 5 gal solar shower bag and a bucket. the shower bag is just a trickle and this is so much better. My one huge complaint is that i've only used it about once a week and one battery wont charge.  I can't seem to locate where to get replacement batteries.  My intention with it was to use it in my van in the spring when I go full time vanlife. kinda bummed about that.

data.title: Battery not waterproof.
data.text: Shower works great my only problem is that the battery is not waterproof. Battery got wet and would not shut off with switch so I had to unplug it. After drying it out really good the switch started working again. So now I keep it in a plastic baggy. Being you have to work the switch in the shower it should be waterproof.

data.title: Decent pump
data.text: I have been using this for 10 - 15 minutes daily for 4 months in an off grid experiment. It works very well for taking a shower or rinsing things off. The spray and volume of water are good. Only problem is recently the pump will shut off during use. I believe it is a problem with the battery switch. All in all its good for intermittent use but ymmv on longevity. I would buy it again if I couldn't find a better alternative.Update: after about 5 months the pump gave up. I haven't tried to take it apart yet to determine the point of failure. I really liked this thing and would consider replacing it with like unit if it had lasted longer or was less expensive. I'm up in the air about recommending it. I originally thought the problem was a battery but both batteries do the same thing and I think it unlikely both are bad.

On the other hand the shower head attachment is terrible and the head can simply fall off the pipe.Oh, and while plugged into a charger the batteries have an incredibly bright blue light. data.title: Great off grid shower!
data.text: I bought this about a year ago and have used it a few times, for off grid camping and washing the dog.  I made one little change...I bought a residential shower head with an on/off button.  Fortunately those are universal and it fit on the Kedsun hose.  Now I can turn off the water without pressing the button on the battery.  Love that the battery is separate from the pump and isn't submerged.  Decent water pressure, very quiet, and has held up for a year.  I use less than a gallon of water to shower with the ability to turn off the water at the shower head, closer to two gallons if I'm washing my hair.  Don't know how long it will last but I will definitely buy again if my current one dies.

data.title: Work well, can recommend
data.text: Works great, with enough water pressure to get the job done. This is perfect for a family member who has mobility issues and will have to bathe using a bench in the tub since we don’t have a shower. The long cord to the battery means the battery can stay reassuringly far away from the water. I have to drop 1 star for the spray nozzle piece, because the lever feels fragile and I’m not confident it can hold up to a lot of use; but overall everything works, and I’m happy with the purchase.

data.title: Works well in both shower and spray modes
data.text: Bought this for quick showers when off grid camping. I use a 5 gallon bucket and this will easily pull the water from ground level to the top of the hose and still have decent pressure. This has 2 nozzle types one is a standard shower head, the other is a trigger operated spray head.  The shower head doesn't have an off function like the spray head does I think that would be a real nice feature so I find on the shower head it kind of wastes water. Now this can be recharged with a USB power bank or even a small solar panel. I was easily able to take 2 full showers with one battery and it said there was still 3/4 power left and it also comes with 2 batteries. Now about the batteries. They use a proprietary connection so you have to use the special USB charging cable they give you to charge them......there is only 1 cable provided so don't lose or break it or . I have not been able to figure out if there is any way to get a second or replacement charging cable. Also the batteries are not waterproof but I would say if you splash them by accident; and yes I have done that they should be fine, just don't drop them into the water bucket. So 4 stars due to the special charging cable that is needed and that they only provide one of those cables.

data.title: Works just like advertised! ZOMBIES!
data.text: Took my first shower outdoors, and I gotta say the pump works like I'm in the shower! A few things though,I don't like the hook design as it doesn't lock the shower head correctly and the suction hanger for if you want to hang it in your bathroom also is pretty flimsy. I found myself showering with it while holding it with one hand. It doesn't replace modern showers but it gets the job done more or less.Charging the batteries were easy as pie, just plug in and wait until the lights stop flashing and when its fully charged, it stops flashing. However, you will have to keep taking the charger out and putting it back in if you want full power. I found myself doing this two to three times for maximum charge.Other than that, it still is a solid product that I would be happy to use even during a zombie apocalypse  :D

data.title: My review/take on this
data.text: EDIT/ADD-ON: So I am still using this thing and it didn't shut off on me, but it stopped pumping, I could hear the little engine going, but no water pumping. I was almost done with my shower and just needed to rinse off. I read on a very HELPFUL review that same thing happened and it might have been the temperature of the engine/water. between 50F to 122F is able to work. I did not have it it higher then 122F because that is way to hot for me. How ever,,,,while I was getting the water ready, I was pouring into the buck with the pump in it with hot water, keep in mind it is a 5 gallon bucket with 3 gallons of cold water first. It might have just over heated via usage. I did try to take it apart thinking it was clogged, but I couldn't figure out how to take it apart without damaging it, it seemed. So I saw that review and just now tried it in some cold water and it works. Will update it need.Ok, so far so good. Got to use it today. Suction power awesome, battery life, I guess great for now. I did make sure to charge one battery fully before testing anything out. I do have concerns though. The battery has a label stating that it is not water proof at all. Which is weird because it is hard to turn off during the use. The button isn't that hard to press but you have to have a grip on it a bit to hold down the button to turn on and to turn off. Wish there was another way. I don't like the fact the port and the charger isn't some style of USB C type or micro USB. Maybe future models will. Thinking this just in case the charger gets lost and or the batter is Unreliable and or un-replaceable, then you have a worthless thing because you can't get power. Again with the power switch, wish it was easier to turn on and off. I did how ever have two 5 gallon buckets ready to test/use and made it easy to take a full shower without running out of warm water to fast. All so since the spray hose nozzle is an on off, but the shower head doesn't have that, will it hurt the motor of the suction engine?

data.title: Worked great creating a camp sink. But could only get one battery to work. Thus the four stars.
data.text: Our campsite spigot was a couple sites away so we had to haul water. So we created a camp sink. We use a 5 gallon bucket. A hole was cut in the center of the lid and dropped the pump in and the hose through the hole. The spay nozzle sat on the table along with a couple of collapsible sinks. When you needed water you just pushed the button on the battery pack to get the pump going and we had running water. When you were done, push the button to turn off the pump. One battery lasted a couple of days.  The only complaint I have is that one of the battery packs would not function. We charged it before leaving and it would not work. It charged but did not function. The light was on continuously and would not turn off. Contacted Amazon and they are sending out a replacement. Hope both work in the next one. Otherwise, great addition to our camping gear.

data.title: Seems to be of quality
data.text: The materials- while plastic seem sturdy and well constructed.  The battery was this powerful ( see video) after about an hour charge.  Nice pressure.  We will charge both batteries overnight before we take off camping.  I saw one review about needing to blow thru the hose if the draw gets sluggish due to air pockets and that did work when I switched from the shower sprayer to the pressure wash nozzle and had inadvertently lifted the pump out of the water.  After I blew from the nozzle end of the tube ( nozzle off, of course) into the water bucket, the pump worked perfectly again.  This, and a camp shower / potty tent were the last things we needed and now we have everything we need to be at home out in the woods.  I should point out that the shower nozzle just starts flowing immediately upon starting the battery whereas the sink-style pressure nozzle only flows with the lever pressed, same as your sink sprayer at home.  After we actually use it camping, i'm hoping to come back and 5 star this rating.  We may need a second one to keep in the camp kitchen, although this is very easily portable enough to use at both places.  I'll find a small plastic covered tote bin to store the sprayer kit with hose, nozzles, batteries, pump, and hanging hardware or maybe just a 5 gallon bucket which would also hold hygiene products for the "bathroom" tent  There are spare o- rings too.

data.title: Pleasantly surprised, but make sure you purge the air by giving it a little shake first.
data.text: We love to go camping but my wife absolutely must have a shower available to her or at least the ability to wash her hair.  So we gave this shower a try and for the money it can't be beat!  We took a 5-Gallon bucket, but she only used about 2.5-3 Gallons to shampoo and condition her hair altogether.  We didn't use the big round shower head, just the little trigger-head which I think gives a little bit more control.I think a lot of people get frustrated because at times it doesn't seem to pump... it's important that you have to find a way to "prime" the pump to get water to get into the pump itself before it'll start to push water, if not then it's just spinning in air and not getting anything to move.To prime the pump, once it's submerged in water, turn it upside down and give it a shake to get the air bubbles out... then when you turn it on, you can hear the tone change and you should have water.  If not, then while hitting the trigger use something... your mouth... whatever to suck water through for a moment and you'll get water flowing.  It's just fluid-dynamics and this is a cheap pump... you can't expect it to be self-priming.Either way, did the job great and my wife definitely feels more comfortable doing a longer trip.

data.title: Was 2 Stars but now 4.
data.text: Updated Review: the priming issue that was in my original review must have been a temporary fluke.  For the last year we have used this shower pump many times and it has served us well.  Good preasure and flow and batteries are still holding up well.Original Review: I received the pump in a timely manner and the packaging / pump were in good order.  Charged batteries and assembled the unit and tested it.  Test went well and I got the pressure I was expecting.  Having used it a few times in conjuncture with some water Jerry cans it generally works ok.  The only complaint, and is significant, is that when the pump looses prime it can have a really hard time getting it back.  I removed the sediment filter and that seemed to improve the issue a little bit.  I am pumping clean water so I'm not worried about anything getting in the pump. data.title: Next Level Camping
data.text: Having certain amenities of home at the camp site really makes the whole experience of camping that much more enjoyable. This battery powered pump is definitely one of those welcomed amenities! All in all this is a good product. I do HIGHLY recommend breaking in the battery before your first excursion into the wilderness. What do I mean? Charge the batteries out of the box (red light will turn blue) and then run the batteries all the way down. Then charge them again. I was fortunate enough to be car camping with an inverter, so I was able to recharge the batteries, but the first battery runtime DID NOT last nearly as long as the following charges. I also recommend heating up water on a stove before washing dishes, to help dissolve the grease on your dishes. Some complaints: I wish this pump had a built in pressure gauge that would automatically shut off the pump when the water wasn't being used. Most RV water pumps have this feature and would be welcomed on this product to save battery life. 2. I wish there was an option to run the pump off of a car's cigarette lighter, instead of just the portable batteries. 3. The included clips are clumsy and not well designed. I wish they was more thought put into them, so the faucet sprayer was able to be securely mounted to the side of a plastic storage container or a 5 gallon utility pale. Other than these complaints, this is a worthy purchase at 50 bucks. Remember to drain and recharge the batteries at least once every 3 months or the batteries will dry out! Nothing new

data.userName: Anime, Manga and Harlequin Romance Girl
data.text: I bought this portable camp shower as an emergency if we lost power and have an easier way to take a shower for myself and for my kids. It turns out I was also able to use it for other things such as helping my husband water his garden. I've been using this camp shower for more than two weeks now so I have gained insight as to how strong the water pressure was, tips and tricks and all . first off I was little wary of the price cuz it is a bit high. However after looking at all the other options this camp shower looked to be the best of all so I sucked it up and bought it. They provide you with two batteries which is great. The fact that the battery pack and water vacuum part is separated is very beneficial. I don't have to worry about the battery pack getting wet as I can just stick it in my pocket. The next thing I like about this is that it works well for my purposes which is to shower and helping to water the garden. I did test the shower head just to see how strong it was and the water pressure was not that strong as a normal shower obviously but it was alright for emergencies. Now the other shower head had much stronger water pressure and this is what I used to help water to Garden. Frankly, if you wanted a stronger water pressure for showering this other shower head might be the one you would prefer to use. I used a stronger water pressure shower head for gardening because I wanted the ability for the water to travel far as we had several garden beds. The second shower head accomplished that as the water pressure was stronger than the first shower head. here are some dislike or trouble I had but these were pretty minor to me. In the beginning I could not figure out why the water would not come out sometimes when I dropped the water pump thing into the bucket and turned it on It would sputter and I had to turn it on and off and then it would work. It turns out there's a trick to this. When you drop the water pump vacuum thingy into the bucket you have to press the shower head and don't release you're hold on it as you turn the shower on. Other wish list I would have liked what does that mean for the hoses and cords to be longer. It would also have been nice if the charging cable was a USB. But other than this minor pet peeves I still really like this camp shower and if it lasts for a long time even better and it would justify the $50 price. I will update this review if anything else occurs but so far I am very happy with discount shower.

data.title: WONDERFUL FOR WATERING THE GARDEN👍👍👍
data.text: This lightweight sprayer is the best thing that I’ve purchased in a long time.  I collect rain water and have to haul it to the garden up hill no less. I use my car so it’s easier for me.  Before I got this sprayer, I had to fill a watering can over and over and over, taking at least 2 to 3 hours to water my garden and other areas with flowers etc.  Now all I have to do is fill up 5gal buckets that are strategically placed throughout the garden areas, drop in the pump, put the battery pack in my pocket and water away.  This sprayer has reduced my watering time by 50% as well as water wastage. I put a fine filter sock around the pump for debris, ensuring that the pump keeps free of large and smaller particles. EXCELLENT OVER ALL PRODUCT, HIGHLY RECOMMEND. The batter life with constant use is an hour plus, also the fact that the batter IS NOT LOCATED ON THE SUBMERSIBLE PUMP makes it another valued bonus as you can put the battery in your pocket ensuring that it stays out of the water ensuring longer battery life..  It takes about 1 1/2 hours to fully charge the battery AND it comes with 2 batteries which is another fabulous bonus.  The hose is about 6’ long and is very easy to put on the pump.  Watering is a pleasure now instead of a timely chore. Excellent value and an excellent price👍👍👍👍👍👍

data.title: Great Little Portable Shower
data.text: My old solar bag shower finally gave out and I purchased this instead.  For camping, or rinsing off dishes or at the beach, this is portable, lightweight and perfect.  The remote battery and switch make this easy to turn on and off.  It's also nice that you don't have to lift a heavy container of water over your head.For a warm shower, just heat the water ahead of time on a camp stove and add that to your container.  The drop-in pump diameter is small enough to fit inside most wide-mouth water containers, so you don't have to place it in an open bucket.The only downside to this unit is the electrical cord and hose are on the short side.  If you want the nozzle over your head, and you're 6 feet or taller, this is a problem.  The alternative is to place your water container on something like a camp chair or stool, or you can somehow get a longer hose and extension wire for this.The pump produces a pretty good stream of water, so it will exhaust your supply quickly, if you simply let it run.  But overall, it's a great product.
